objective: For: turning one raw talking-to-camera clip into a post-ready ≤60s short-form edit for socials.
Fires when: user hands over raw talking-head footage asking to remove mistakes/awkwardness or 'make this a reel'. NOT for long-form, no-speech, multi-cam/b-roll, or in-Vyra editing sessions.

Wedge: editorial (not mechanical) cutting — retakes/flubs judged from the transcript (keep last complete take), coherence-gated tightening to ≤60s, conservative hook pass — ending in a Premiere Pro 2022-importable deliverable, not a roughcut.

Produces: <clip>-edit/ folder — sequence.xml (FCP7 XML, 9:16 @ source fps, all cuts applied, non-destructive), captions.srt (short cues, re-timed), edit-report.md (every cut: timecode + reason-class; kept transcript; final runtime).
inPractice: Triggers on raw talking-head footage + a make-it-postable ask: 'clean up this raw video for socials', 'turn this raw take into a reel', 'edit out my bad takes', 'get this down to 60s for TikTok'.

Stays quiet on: long-form/podcast silence-stripping (redirects to auto-editor-class tools), no-speech video, interviews/multi-cam/b-roll, repurposing finished long videos, in-Vyra sessions, caption-only asks.
Preflights deps (ffprobe, faster-whisper), transcribes word-level, states the core message, then cuts by reason-class: mistakes (keep LAST complete take), false starts, filler, dead air.
Coherence gate before anything is generated; >60s → fixed time-ladder (redundancy → tangent → weakest support), core message never cut; hook pass prefers cutting preamble over reordering.
Delivers <clip>-edit/: sequence.xml (FCP7 XML, 1080×1920 @ source fps, self-checked), captions.srt (re-timed), edit-report.md (every cut: timecode + reason + ⚠ flags), cutlist.json.
Guards: never cuts mid-word or anything it can't read confidently; non-destructive (never touches the source, never renders); user prefs override all defaults.

shape.rationale: One artifact chain in one sitting; no pipeline to join; XML emitter is a script asset, not a sibling skill.

manifest.item: Cut-decision rubric incl. keep-last-good-take retake logic
Hook pass (reorder when it improves the edit)
Edit report with cuts + reasons + runtime

manifest.note: standalone; nothing composes with it yet

uniqueWedge: Unlike auto-editor/ButterCut-style roughcut and silence-cut tools, raw-to-reel makes editorial judgments from the transcript — drops flubbed/repeated takes keeping the best one, tightens to ≤60s through a coherence gate so the survivor still makes sense, and leads with the hook — delivering a post-ready 9:16 Premiere sequence + captions rather than a roughcut.

annotations: [!NEEDS-ASSET] FCP7 XML generator script is load-bearing; Build must produce and test it
[!NEEDS-ASSET] FCP7 XML generator must be verified against Premiere importer shape
boundary: in-Vyra sessions → Vyra skills; mechanical-silence-only asks → point at auto-editor-class tools

scripts: make_sequence.py (smoke-tested: NTSC frame math, reorder re-timing, BOM tolerance, pathurl fixed)

annotations: [!NEEDS-ASSET]-RESOLVED — XML generator built and smoke-tested against synthetic cutlist

arena.selfRepair: description gained interview/multi-speaker + finished-long-video boundary; arena re-run clean

decisions.chosen: Premiere Pro 2022 via FCP7 XML interchange + SRT (local ffmpeg+whisper pipeline, no Vyra dependency)
decisions.rationale: User asked for Premiere output; .prproj is undocumented — FCP7 XML is the reliable, importable interchange.
User selected all four polish options.

decisions.chosen: cut weakest content automatically until ≤60s, preserving core message
keep LAST complete take; fall back to most complete if last is broken
decisions.rationale: Creators re-record until they nail it; completeness check guards the exception.

decisions.chosen: never mid-word; ~150-200ms padding; frame-snapped; straight jump-cuts
decisions.rationale: Short-form aesthetic accepts jump-cuts; padding prevents clipped syllables and robotic pacing.

decisions.chosen: conservative reorder — only when clearly stronger AND seam survives coherence gate; else cut preamble instead
decisions.rationale: Reordering risks continuity; cutting throat-clearing achieves the same hook most of the time.

decisions.chosen: 1080x1920 @ source fps; scale-to-fill-height center crop; user adjusts in Premiere
decisions.rationale: Sane default; framing taste belongs to the user in the NLE.

decisions.chosen: SRT, short cues (≤4 words, ≥0.5s), re-timed to edited sequence; styling left to Premiere
decisions.rationale: SRT is what Premiere 2022 ingests; short cues match short-form pacing.

decisions.decision: Ideation B1 accepted: optional phone preview render
decisions.chosen: P8 + scripts/render_preview.py (540x960, captions burned in, offered at handover)
decisions.rationale: User opted in at final gate; delta re-verified PASS.

decisions.decision: Ideation C1 accepted: post kit in report
decisions.chosen: caption + 3 titles + hashtags, grounded in kept transcript only
decisions.rationale: User opted in at final gate; delta re-verified PASS.
